raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "asserting personal agency and self-responsibility as a guiding principle for behavior",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "individuals possess primary control over their own identity and development",
    "external influences are secondary to internal choices",
    "self-creation is an ongoing, deliberate process"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent as a claim of radical self-authorship, though it elides degrees of external constraint",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the listener has sufficient agency and resources to act on self-directed change, and the statement is used to encourage accountability rather than blame",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "structural, social, or biological factors heavily limit self-determination, rendering the claim of sole authorship factually inaccurate or psychologically harmful"
}
